FR-4  Profile Updates Customizing identity and family roles. FR-16  Age Classification Dynamic age group mapping. NFR-1  Zero Trust Mandatory session validation for all APIs.

docs.title: 3. Shopping List Module

FR-11  Data Retention Automatic purging of old data (3 months). FR-12  Loyalty Cards Digital storage with QR and Code 128 support. FR-15  Expiration Warnings Proactive alerts for expiring coupons.

docs.title: 4. Settings & Shared Patterns

FR-42  Centralized Media Service Unified photo storage and serving across all modules. NFR-2  Latency Target 95% of requests processed in < 150ms.

docs.title: 5. Documentation System
docs.text: ID Requirement Description FR-20  MkDocs Integrated documentation-as-code build. NFR-5  Portability Static site generation for easy hosting.

docs.title: 6. Recipes & Meals Module
docs.text: ID Requirement Description FR-23  Recipe Management CRUD for recipes with markdown, labels, and metadata. FR-24  Recipe Photos Multi-photo upload via centralized media service with default photo selection. FR-25  Recipe Ingredients Link shopping items as ingredients with quantities and optional grouping. FR-26  Nutrition Data Per-item nutrition via nutrient master catalog with on-the-fly recipe totals. FR-27  Preparation Steps Ordered steps with drag-and-drop reordering. FR-28  Recipe Comments Household members can comment on recipes. FR-29  Recipe Ratings 1-5 star ratings with average and individual votes. FR-30  Meal Time Configuration Configurable meal times with per-day schedules. FR-31  Weekly Meal Plan Monday–Sunday planner with multi-recipe meals, standalone items, and multipliers. FR-32  Meal Plan Publish & Notify Publish plan and notify household (approval workflow dropped). FR-33  Meal Reminders Per-assignment preparation reminders. Deferred — not yet functional. FR-34  Meal Plan Retention Auto-purge plans older than 10 weeks. FR-35  Meals This Week Dashboard widget and dedicated weekly view. FR-36  Shopping List Integration Export meal ingredients to shopping lists. FR-37  Meal Thumbs Up/Down Quick feedback via dedicated votes table. FR-40  Nutrient Master Catalog Predefined nutrient catalog for structured nutrition tracking. FR-41  Standalone Meal Items Assign shopping items directly to meal entries without recipes.

docs.title: 7. Notifications & Messaging
docs.text: ID Requirement Description FR-38  In-App Notifications Notification bell with typed events and unread count. FR-39  User Messaging Direct messaging between household members.

docs.title: 8. Glossary
docs.text: Term Definition HATEOAS Hypermedia as the Engine of Application State. RFC 7807 Standard for Problem Details for HTTP APIs. Global Last Price The most recent price recorded for an item across all stores. Meal Plan A weekly schedule (Monday–Sunday) assigning recipes and items to meal times and household members. Meal Plan Entry Item A standalone shopping item assigned directly to a meal entry without requiring a recipe. Nutrient A predefined nutrition metric (e.g., Energy, Protein, Fat) from the managed recipes.nutrients catalog, with a name, unit, and description. Nutrition Data Numeric nutrition entries per shopping item, each referencing a nutrient from the master catalog with a value (e.g., Protein: 23.4). Media Service The centralized photo storage system (media.photos table) serving binary images via /api/images/{name} across all modules. Recipe Label A dynamic tag attached to recipes for categorization (e.g., "Vegetarian", "Quick"). Created on demand, auto-deleted when orphaned. Meal Time A named eating occasion (e.g., Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner) with configurable times per day of the week. Meal Plan Vote A thumbs-up or thumbs-down reaction stored in meal_plan_votes, one per user per meal entry.

docs.title: 9. Architectural Decision Records (ADRs)
docs.text:
ADR-1: HATEOAS for REST API

Status: Accepted. Uses Spring HATEOAS to ensure discoverability and decouple the client from hardcoded URI patterns.

ADR-2: Monorepo with Gradle

Status: Accepted.

Uses a single repository to manage backend, frontend, and documentation for atomic releases and shared tooling.

ADR-3: Multi-Recipe Meals

Status: Accepted.

A meal plan entry can combine multiple recipes (e.g., a protein recipe plus a salad recipe) to form a complete meal. This provides flexibility without requiring a separate "meal" abstraction layer.

ADR-4: Dynamic Labels with Auto-Cleanup

Status: Accepted.

Recipe labels are created on demand when a user types a new one, with autocomplete from existing labels. When a label is removed from a recipe and no other recipe references it, the label is automatically deleted. No predefined seed data.

ADR-5: On-the-Fly Nutrition Calculation

Status: Accepted.

Recipe nutrition totals are computed at query time by summing each ingredient's nutrition data multiplied by its quantity. This avoids denormalized storage and ensures totals are always accurate when nutrition data on items changes.

ADR-6: Centralized Media Service

Status: Accepted.

All photos (profile, recipe, item, store) are stored in a single media.photos table with binary data (BYTEA) and served via /api/images/{name}. This replaces per-table Base64 storage, enabling consistent photo handling, deduplication, and efficient binary serving with proper Content-Type headers.

ADR-7: Predefined Nutrient Catalog

Status: Accepted.

Nutrition entries reference a managed recipes.nutrients master table instead of using free-form key-value-unit triples. This ensures consistency across items, enables validation, and supports a settings UI for managing available nutrients. The system ships with 17 seeded nutrients (Energy, Fat, Protein, etc.).

docs.title: Requirements: Settings & Configuration
docs.text:
EARS Syntax Legend (Hover for trigger name)

  • Ubiquitous: "The system shall..." (Always true)
  • Event-driven: "When , the system shall..."
  • Unwanted Behavior: "If , then the system shall..."
  • State-driven: "While , the system shall..."
  • Optional: "Where , the system shall..."
  • Complex: Combinations of the above triggers.

docs.title: FR-18: Age Group Configuration
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall support the Child, Teenager, and Adult predefined age groups.
  2. While a user is classified as an Adult, the system shall allow the user to define the min_age and max_age for each age group.
  3. If the user defines overlapping age ranges or ranges that do not cover the 0-120 age span, then the system shall return a validation error.
  4. While a user is NOT classified as an Adult, the system shall deny access to all age group configuration settings.

Rationale

So That age-based classification remains flexible, "Adult" users can customize the age ranges.

docs.title: FR-19: Family Role Configuration
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall protect the Mother, Father, Son, and Daughter roles from deletion or renaming.
  2. While a user is classified as an Adult, the system shall allow the user to create and delete custom family roles.
  3. The system shall ensure that every user is assigned to exactly one family role.
  4. While a user is NOT classified as an Adult, the system shall deny access to all family role management features.

Rationale

So That the family hierarchy is accurately represented, "Adult" users can manage available roles.

docs.title: UJ-5: Configuring Household Age Groups
docs.text:
1. An Adult user navigates to the Settings module.
  2. The user modifies the "Teenager" range from 13-17 to 12-18.
  3. The system validates that the new ranges are contiguous and non-overlapping.
  4. Upon saving, the system triggers a recalculation of age groups for all household members.

docs.title: Requirements: Shared & Global UI
docs.text:
EARS Syntax Legend (Hover for trigger name)

  • Ubiquitous: "The system shall..." (Always true)
  • Event-driven: "When , the system shall..."
  • Unwanted Behavior: "If , then the system shall..."
  • State-driven: "While , the system shall..."
  • Optional: "Where , the system shall..."
  • Complex: Combinations of the above triggers.

docs.title: FR-14: Module Navigation (Nested Menus)
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall provide top-level parent items in the main navigation for each functional module.
  2. When a user selects a parent navigation item, the system shall reveal the nested sub-menu for its internal features.
  3. While a sub-feature is active, the system shall highlight the corresponding navigation item and maintain the expanded state of the parent module menu.

Rationale

So That the interface remains organized as the application grows, all functional modules SHALL follow a consistent navigation pattern.

docs.title: FR-42: Centralized Media Service
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall store all uploaded photos (profile, recipe, item, store) in a centralized media.photos table with binary data (BYTEA), content type, and a unique name identifier.
  2. The system shall serve photos via a public endpoint (GET /api/images/{name}) returning the binary data with the appropriate Content-Type header.
  3. The system shall categorize photos by type (profile, recipe, item, store) for organizational purposes.
  4. When a user uploads a photo in any module, the system shall store it in the centralized media table and return the URL reference for the calling module to persist.

Rationale

So That photo storage is consistent, deduplicated, and efficiently served across all modules, the system uses a single media service rather than per-table Base64 storage.

docs.title: NFR-2: Performance (Latency)
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall complete 95% of standard CRUD API requests within 150ms.
  2. When a user interacts with a UI element, the system shall provide visual feedback within 100ms.

docs.title: Requirements: Shopping List Module
docs.text:
EARS Syntax Legend (Hover for trigger name)

  • Ubiquitous: "The system shall..." (Always true)
  • Event-driven: "When , the system shall..."
  • Unwanted Behavior: "If , then the system shall..."
  • State-driven: "While , the system shall..."
  • Optional: "Where , the system shall..."
  • Complex: Combinations of the above triggers.

docs.title: UJ-3: Collaborative Shopping
docs.text:
1. User A creates a "Weekly Groceries" shopping list and adds "Milk" and "Eggs".
  2. User B logs in from their mobile device and sees the "Weekly Groceries" list on the dashboard.
  3. User B adds "Bread" to the same list while at the office.
  4. User A goes to the store, opens the list, and marks "Milk" as bought.
  5. "Milk" moves to the bottom of the list with a strikethrough, visible to both users in real-time.

docs.title: UJ-4: Using Loyalty Cards & Coupons
docs.text:
1. User A adds a "Dunnes Stores" loyalty card with number 123456789 and selects "Code 128".
  2. User A adds a "€5 Off Groceries" coupon for Dunnes with an expiration date in 3 days and a photo of the voucher.
  3. On the Home Page, a warning panel highlights that the "€5 Off" coupon is expiring soon.
  4. At the checkout, User A opens the Shopping List module, selects Dunnes, and shows the Code 128 barcode to the cashier.
  5. User A then opens the Coupons section, shows the voucher photo, and marks it as "Used" after the discount is applied.

docs.title: FR-5: Category & Item Management
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall allow users to create categories with a name, description, and a library icon.
  2. The system shall allow users to create items with a name, a unit (the default selling unit), and either a library icon or an uploaded photo stored via the centralized media service.
  3. The system shall establish a link between every shopping item and exactly one category.
  4. The system shall enforce a unique constraint on (name, category_id) to prevent duplicate items within the same category.
  5. Where an item is sold in discrete pieces (e.g., a 1L bottle), the system shall allow users to specify pc_quantity and pc_unit for piece-to-unit conversion.
  6. The system shall store nutrition_sample_size (default 100) and nutrition_sample_unit (default 'g') to define the reference portion for nutrition data.

Rationale

So That I can organize my needs, I want to manage shopping categories and master items.

docs.title: FR-6: Store Management
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall allow users to create stores with a name, description, and either a library icon or an uploaded photo.

Rationale

So That I can plan where to shop, I want to manage a list of favorite stores.

docs.title: FR-7: Shopping List Creation & Collaboration
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. While a shopping list exists, the system shall share its visibility and edit permissions with all members of the household.
  2. The system shall display the identity of the user who created the list.
  3. Where a list is marked as "Completed", the system shall hide it from the default active view.

Rationale

So That our household is synchronized, I want to create shared shopping lists.

docs.title: FR-8: List Items & Quantities
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. Where a user adds an item to a list, the system shall allow the selection of a specific store or the "Any Place" designation.
  2. The system shall require the selection of a quantity and a Unit of Measure for each list item.
  3. Where a user provides a price, the system shall record it against the specific list item.

Rationale

So That I know exactly what to buy, I want to specify quantities and stores for list items.

docs.title: FR-9: Price History & Suggestions
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. When a price is recorded for an item at a store, the system shall save it to the permanent price history.
  2. Where a user adds an item for a specific store, the system shall suggest the last price paid for that item at that store.
  3. Where a user adds an item with the "Any Place" designation, the system shall suggest the most recent price paid for that item across all stores.

Rationale

So That I can estimate my spending, I want the system to track prices and suggest them.

docs.title: FR-10: In-Store Progress Tracking
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. When a user marks an item as "Bought", the system shall move the item to the end of the list and apply a strikethrough style to its name.
  2. When a user marks an item as "Unavailable", the system shall visually distinguish it from bought and pending items, indicating the item could not be found at the store.

Rationale

So That I don't miss anything while shopping, I want to track what I've already put in my cart.

docs.title: FR-11: Automatic Data Retention
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall identify shopping lists that are older than 3 months.
  2. When the daily retention task executes, the system shall permanently delete all shopping lists older than 3 months and their associated items.

Rationale

So That the system remains performant and cluttered-free, old shopping lists should be removed automatically.

docs.title: FR-12: Loyalty Cards
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall allow users to associate loyalty cards with specific stores.
  2. While a loyalty card exists, the system shall share it with all members of the household.
  3. The system shall render loyalty card numbers as either a QR Code or a Code 128 barcode upon user request.

Rationale

So That I don't have to carry physical cards, I want to store my loyalty card numbers in the app.

docs.title: FR-13: Store Coupons
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall allow users to store coupons with a name, description, value, photo, due date, and an optional barcode (code + type).
  2. Where a coupon has a barcode configured, the system shall render it as either a QR Code or a Code 128 barcode for scanning at checkout.
  3. When a user marks a coupon as "Used", the system shall hide it from all default active views.

Rationale

So That I can save money, I want to manage my digital and physical coupons.

docs.title: FR-15: Expiration Warning Panel (Dashboard)
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. While a coupon is NOT marked as "Used" and its due date is within 4 days, the system shall display it in the "Expiring Coupons" dashboard panel.

Rationale

So That I don't forget to use my rewards, I want a prominent reminder of expiring coupons.

docs.title: NFR-3: Reliability & Sync
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. While the mobile UI is offline, the system shall allow users to view and check off items.
  2. When internet connectivity is restored, the system shall automatically synchronize offline changes with the server.

docs.title: NFR-4: Accessibility & UX
docs.text:
Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall provide a minimum tap target size of 44x44px for all interactive elements in the shopping list interface.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
In the Spec-Driven Development (SDD) workflow, task files represent the bridge between high-level architectural design and the actual code implementation. They serve as the definitive "Execution Plan," breaking down complex features into transactional, verifiable units of work that ensure the final product aligns perfectly with the established requirements.

Each task file is structured to guide the developer through a deterministic implementation path, starting from environment setup and schema migrations to backend logic, frontend components, and finally, automated verification. This methodology eliminates ambiguity during the build phase and provides a clear audit trail for progress tracking and quality assurance.

docs.title: Task Lifecycle
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    Design["<b>Design</b><br/>Architecture & Patterns"]
    Tasks["<b>Tasking</b><br/>Execution Plan"]
    Implement["<b>Implementation</b><br/>Code & Tests"]
    Validate["<b>Validation</b><br/>Verification against Spec"]

    Design --> Tasks
    Tasks --> Implement
    Implement --> Validate
    Validate -- "Feedback" --> Design

docs.title: Active Roadmap
docs.text:
These modules are currently defined and follow the sequential rollout plan:

Milestone Module Status 01 User Profile Updates Completed 02 Shopping List Module Completed 03 Family Hierarchy Completed 04 Recipes & Meals Completed

docs.title: Implementation Notes (Milestone 04)
docs.text:
Scope Changes During Implementation

The following deviations from the original specification occurred during Milestone 04:

  • FR-32 (Meal Plan Approval Workflow): Intentionally dropped. The per-member PENDING/ACCEPTED/CHANGED tracking was deemed too complex. Only the publish & notify flow was implemented.
  • FR-33 (Meal Preparation Reminders): Deferred. The MealReminderScheduler service is scaffolded but the reminder_offset_minutes column is not yet in the schema.
  • New features added: Centralized media service (FR-42), nutrient master catalog (FR-40), standalone meal items (FR-41), ingredient grouping, recipe multipliers, coupon barcodes, item unavailability.

docs.title: Backlog
docs.text:
These are candidate features that have been identified for future implementation but have not yet been formalized into requirements or design specs.

Meal Preparation Reminders (FR-33)

Complete the meal reminder implementation: add reminder_offset_minutes column to meal_plan_entries, wire the MealReminderScheduler to query upcoming meals and generate MEAL_REMINDER notifications.

Health Tracking

A new module to track health mtrics such as weight and measures. ideally integrated with apple/google health.

Family Calendar

A shared household calendar for tracking events, appointments, and important family dates, with integration for automated reminders.

Household Tasks

A collaborative task manager for managing recurring household chores, maintenance schedules, and shared responsibilities.

Family Wish List

A centralized list for tracking desired items, gift ideas, and future purchases, allowing family members to contribute and coordinate.

Finances Planning

A comprehensive budget and expense tracking module to manage common household costs, savings goals, and shared bills.

Travel Management

A coordination hub for family trips, including itinerary planning, document storage (passports, tickets), and shared packing lists.

Licenses & Warranty Database

A secure vault for tracking software licenses, appliance warranties, and subscription renewal dates.

Shared Passwords

A highly secure, encrypted storage for common household credentials such as streaming services, utilities, and insurance portals.

K8s Deployment Module

A specialized Gradle module to manage Infrastructure-as-Code for deploying the entire application stack to a Kubernetes cluster.

docs.title: 1. Overview
docs.text:
The goal of this epic is to enable authenticated users to update their profile information and to provide a detailed "Quick View" of this information in the application header.

Status Legend

  • Completed
  • In Progress
  • Planned

docs.title: US-1: Backend - Implement Profile Update API
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a home user, I want to securely update my profile via a REST API, so that my social and contact details are stored and shared within the application.

  • Persona: Backend Engineer
  • Estimate: 4 hours
  • Dependencies: None

Validates Requirements

  • FR-4: Update User Profile
  • NFR-1: Security (Zero Trust)

Acceptance Criteria

  1. PUT /api/user/me endpoint exists and is secured by Spring Security.
  2. Endpoint accepts a JSON body matching the UserProfileUpdateRequest schema.
  3. Returns 200 OK with the updated HATEOAS-compliant user profile.
  4. If invalid phone numbers or social links are provided, then the system shall return 400 Bad Request with RFC 7807 details.
  5. The system shall prevent updates to email, firstName, and lastName.
  6. If the database is unreachable, then the system shall return 503 Service Unavailable.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Add putUserProfile method to UserProfileController.
  • [x] Add updateProfile method to UserProfileService.
  • [x] Ensure UserProfileDTO validation annotations are correctly triggered.
  • [x] Implement UserProfileUpdateSpec.groovy integration test.

docs.title: US-2: Frontend - Implement Profile Edit UI
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a home user, I want an intuitive interface to edit my profile, so that I can easily update my details without technical knowledge.

  • Persona: Frontend Engineer
  • Estimate: 4 hours
  • Dependencies: US-1

Validates Requirements

  • FR-4: Update User Profile

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When the user selects the profile menu, the system shall navigate to the Profile Edit page.
  2. The system shall display existing profile data with email, firstName, and lastName marked as read-only.
  3. Where a user provides a local file, the system shall upload it as a profile photo.
  4. When a user enters data, the system shall provide real-time validation for phone and social URLs.
  5. The system shall show a success notification upon a successful profile update.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Add updateUserProfile function to src/services/api.ts.
  • [x] Create ProfilePage.tsx using useForm from @mantine/form.
  • [x] Implement useMutation for profile updates.
  • [x] Add routing for the new Profile page in App.tsx.

docs.title: US-3: Frontend - Enhance Profile Dropdown UI
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a home user, I want to see my contact and social info in the header menu, so that I can quickly access these details.

  • Persona: Frontend Engineer
  • Estimate: 2 hours
  • Dependencies: US-1

Validates Requirements

  • FR-3: User Profile Quick View

Acceptance Criteria

  1. While data is available, the system shall display the phone number and social icons in the dropdown.
  2. When a user clicks a social icon, the system shall open the link in a new tab.
  3. The system shall provide a link to "View/Edit Profile" within the dropdown menu.
  4. Where profile data is missing, the system shall hide the corresponding sections in the dropdown.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Update Layout.tsx with conditional rendering logic.
  • [x] Integrate Tabler icons into the menu.
  • [x] Link the menu item to the /profile route.

docs.title: 3. Definition of Done (DoD)
docs.text:
Quality Checklist

  • [x] Code follows project conventions (Java 25, React 19, Spock).
  • [x] All unit and integration tests are passing.
  • [x] RFC 7807 compliance for all error responses.
  • [x] Traceability between requirements, design, and implementation is maintained.

docs.title: 1. Overview
docs.text:
Implement a comprehensive shopping assistant module that allows household members to manage categories, items, stores, and collaborative shopping lists, complete with loyalty cards and coupon tracking.

Status Legend

  • Completed
  • In Progress
  • Planned

docs.title: US-1: Master Data - Categories & Items
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to manage my own categories and items, so that I can organize my shopping needs according to my preferences.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 6 hours
  • Dependencies: None

Validates Requirements

  • FR-5: Category & Item Management

Acceptance Criteria

  1. CRUD operations for Shopping Categories (Name, Description, Icon).
  2. CRUD operations for Shopping Items (Name, Photo, Category).
  3. Every item is linked to a category.
  4. If a category name is not unique, then the system shall return a validation error.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for shopping_categories and shopping_items.
  • [x] Implement JPA Entities and Repositories for Category and Item.
  • [x] Implement REST Controllers with HATEOAS support.
  • [x] Build React management pages using Mantine.

docs.title: US-2: Store Management - Loyalty & Coupons
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to manage stores, loyalty cards, and coupons, so that I can save money and shop efficiently.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 8 hours
  • Dependencies: US-1

Validates Requirements

  • FR-6: Store Management
  • FR-12: Loyalty Cards
  • FR-13: Store Coupons

Acceptance Criteria

  1. CRUD for Stores (Name, Description, Icon, Photo).
  2. Add/Display Loyalty Cards with QR/Code 128 rendering.
  3. Manage Coupons with due dates, photos, and "Used" status.
  4. While a loyalty card exists, the system shall share it with the entire household.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for shopping_stores, loyalty_cards, and coupons.
  • [x] Implement JPA Entities and HATEOAS Controllers.
  • [x] Integrate react-barcode and qrcode.react libraries.
  • [x] Build Store details view with nested sections.

docs.title: US-3: Shopping Lists - Planning & Suggestions
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to create and plan shared shopping lists with price suggestions, so that my family is synchronized on what to buy.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 8 hours
  • Dependencies: US-2

Validates Requirements

  • FR-7: Shopping List Collaboration
  • FR-8: List Items & Quantities
  • FR-9: Price History & Suggestions

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Create shared shopping lists with a "Completed" status.
  2. Add items to lists with quantity, unit (L, kg, etc.), and optional price.
  3. Where a user adds an item, the system shall suggest the last price paid at the selected store.
  4. Lists shall display the user who created them.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for shopping_lists and shopping_list_items.
  • [x] Implement Price Suggestion logic in ShoppingService.
  • [x] Build "Create/Edit List" UI with searchable item selection.
  • [x] Implement real-time list sharing via TanStack Query.

docs.title: US-4: Shopping Mode - In-Store Experience
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a shopper, I want to check off items in real-time while at the store, so that I don't miss anything and others know what was bought.

  • Persona: Frontend Engineer
  • Estimate: 6 hours
  • Dependencies: US-3

Validates Requirements

  • FR-10: In-Store Progress Tracking
  • NFR-3: Reliability & Sync

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When a user marks an item as "Bought", the system shall move it to the bottom with a strikethrough.
  2. The interface shall be mobile-optimized with a minimum 44x44px tap target.
  3. While offline, the system shall support viewing and checking items.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Implement "Shopping Mode" UI optimized for mobile devices.
  • [x] Implement optimistic updates for check-off actions.
  • [x] Add completed_at timestamp logic for list finalization.

docs.title: US-5: Global Maintenance & Warnings
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a system user, I want to be warned about expiring coupons and have old data purged, so that the app remains relevant and fast.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 4 hours
  • Dependencies: US-2, US-4

Validates Requirements

  • FR-11: Automatic Data Retention
  • FR-15: Expiration Warning Panel

Acceptance Criteria

  1. While a coupon is due within 4 days, the system shall display it in the "Expiring Coupons" dashboard panel.
  2. The side navigation shall use a nested "Shopping" menu for all sub-features.
  3. When the daily retention task runs, the system shall delete lists older than 3 months.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Implement Spring @Scheduled task for 3-month data retention.
  • [x] Build the "Expiring Coupons" widget for the Dashboard.
  • [x] Refactor global navigation to use nested structures.

docs.title: 3. Definition of Done (DoD)
docs.text:
Quality Checklist

  • [x] Code follows project conventions (Java 25, React 19, Spock).
  • [x] All unit and integration tests (TS-13 to TS-17) are passing.
  • [x] RFC 7807 compliance for all new shopping APIs.
  • [x] Traceability between requirements, design, and implementation is maintained.

docs.title: 1. Overview
docs.text:
Implement the foundational layers for family roles and age-based classification, including Google birthdate synchronization and a configuration settings module.

Status Legend

  • Completed
  • In Progress
  • Planned

docs.title: US-1: Backend - Family Hierarchy Schema & Entities
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a system architect, I want to persist family roles and age group configurations, so that users can be classified within the household.

  • Persona: Backend Engineer
  • Estimate: 4 hours
  • Dependencies: None

Validates Requirements

  • FR-16: Automated Age Group Classification
  • FR-18: Age Group Configuration

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When the migration runs, the database tables family_roles and age_group_config shall be created.
  2. The user_profile table shall include birthdate, family_role_id, and age_group_name.
  3. JPA entities for FamilyRole and AgeGroupConfig shall be implemented.
  4. The UserProfile entity shall maintain @ManyToOne relationships with roles.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration 06-add-family-hierarchy.sql.
  • [x] Implement FamilyRole and AgeGroupConfig entities and repositories.
  • [x] Update UserProfile entity and UserProfileDTO.
  • [x] Run TS-19 (automated classification) unit tests.

docs.title: US-2: Backend - Google Birthdate Sync & Onboarding
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a new user, I want my birthdate to be automatically retrieved from Google, so that my onboarding experience is seamless.

  • Persona: Backend Engineer
  • Estimate: 6 hours
  • Dependencies: US-1

Validates Requirements

  • FR-17: Google Birthdate Integration
  • UJ-1: First-Time Login (Onboarding)

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The system shall request the user.birthday.read scope from Google.
  2. When a user completes onboarding, the system shall call Google People API to retrieve the birthdate.
  3. The system shall automatically assign the "Adult" age group to the very first user.
  4. If Google returns no birthdate, then the system shall prompt for manual entry.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Update SecurityConfig.java and application.yaml with new OAuth2 scopes.
  • [x] Implement GooglePeopleService to fetch birthdays.
  • [x] Update CustomOAuth2UserService to handle age group bootstrapping.
  • [x] Implement AgeClassificationService for range mapping.
  • [x] Verify with TS-18 and TS-20 integration tests.

docs.title: US-3: Backend - Settings API & Access Control
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a parent (Adult), I want to configure household age ranges and roles, so that I can manage my family's structure.

  • Persona: Backend Engineer
  • Estimate: 4 hours
  • Dependencies: US-2

Validates Requirements

  • FR-18: Age Group Configuration
  • FR-19: Family Role Configuration

Acceptance Criteria

  1. GET /api/settings/age-groups and PUT /api/settings/age-groups endpoints implemented.
  2. GET /api/settings/roles endpoint implemented.
  3. 403 Forbidden returned if a non-Adult user accesses Settings.
  4. Age range updates trigger a full household age group recalculation.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Implement SettingsController and SettingsService.
  • [x] Add @PreAuthorize security checks for "Adult" age group.
  • [x] Implement bulk age recalculation logic.
  • [x] Verify with TS-21 integration tests.

docs.title: US-4: Frontend - Profile & Settings UI
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a user, I want to see my age group and role, and manage household settings if I am an adult.

  • Persona: Frontend Engineer
  • Estimate: 6 hours
  • Dependencies: US-3

Validates Requirements

  • FR-4: Update User Profile
  • FR-14: Module Navigation

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Profile page displays birthdate and family role selection.
  2. Main sidebar features a nested "Settings" menu (visible to Adults only).
  3. Settings module allows editing age ranges and adding custom roles.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Update UserProfileDTO and api.ts in frontend.
  • [x] Update ProfilePage.tsx with new fields and validation.
  • [x] Implement SettingsPage.tsx with Mantine components.
  • [x] Implement conditional rendering for Settings menu in Layout.tsx.

docs.title: 3. Definition of Done (DoD)
docs.text:
Quality Checklist

  • [x] Code follows project conventions (Java 25, React 19, Spock).
  • [x] All unit and integration tests (TS-18 to TS-21) are passing.
  • [x] RFC 7807 compliance for all error responses.
  • [x] Traceability between requirements, design, and implementation is maintained.

docs.title: 1. Overview
docs.text:
Implement a comprehensive recipes and meal planning module that allows household members to manage a shared cookbook, plan weekly meals with per-member assignments, and export meal ingredients to shopping lists. Includes an in-app notification and messaging system.

Status Legend

  • Completed
  • In Progress
  • Planned

docs.title: US-1: Recipe CRUD
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to create, view, edit, and delete recipes, so that our family can build a shared cookbook.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 8 hours
  • Dependencies: None

Validates Requirements

  • FR-23: Recipe Management

Acceptance Criteria

  1. CRUD operations for Recipes (name, description in markdown, servings, source link, video link, prep time).
  2. The system shall display the recipe creator's name.
  3. If a recipe is referenced by a meal plan, then the system shall prevent deletion.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for recipes schema and recipes table.
  • [x] Implement JPA Entity, Repository, Service, and DTO for Recipe.
  • [x] Implement REST Controller with HATEOAS support.
  • [x] Build React RecipesListPage, RecipeDetailPage, and RecipeFormPage.

docs.title: US-2: Recipe Photos & Dynamic Labels
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to add photos and labels to my recipes, so that they are visually appealing and easy to find.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 6 hours
  • Dependencies: US-1

Validates Requirements

  • FR-24: Recipe Photos
  • FR-23: Recipe Management (labels)

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Upload one or more Base64-encoded photos per recipe.
  2. Designate a default photo for list views.
  3. When a user types a new label, the system shall create it automatically.
  4. When a label is orphaned, the system shall delete it.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for recipe_photos, labels, and recipe_labels tables.
  • [x] Implement entities, repositories, and services for photos and labels.
  • [x] Implement photo upload/delete and default selection endpoints.
  • [x] Implement label autocomplete endpoint (GET /labels?q=).
  • [x] Build photo gallery and label tag input components.

docs.title: US-3: Ingredients & Nutrition Data
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to add ingredients from the shopping catalog and see nutrition totals, so that I know what I need and can track dietary intake.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 6 hours
  • Dependencies: US-1

Validates Requirements

  • FR-25: Recipe Ingredients
  • FR-26: Nutrition Data

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Add ingredients by selecting items from shopping.shopping_items with quantity and unit (KG, G, L, ML, PACK, UNIT).
  2. When the recipe detail is requested, the system shall calculate nutrition totals on-the-fly from flexible key-value-unit nutrition entries.
  3. If an ingredient has no nutrition entries, the system shall exclude it from totals and indicate the gap.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for recipe_ingredients and nutrition_entries tables.
  • [x] Implement entities, repositories, and services.
  • [x] Implement on-the-fly nutrition calculation in RecipeService (sum entries grouped by nutrient key × ingredient quantity).
  • [x] Implement ingredient CRUD and nutrition GET/PUT endpoints.
  • [x] Build ingredient selector and nutrition summary UI.

docs.title: US-4: Preparation Steps with Drag-and-Drop
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to add ordered preparation steps and reorder them via drag-and-drop, so that anyone can follow the recipe.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 5 hours
  • Dependencies: US-1

Validates Requirements

  • FR-27: Preparation Steps

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Add steps with markdown instruction and optional time_minutes.
  2. Steps are persisted and displayed in sort_order.
  3. When steps are reordered, the system shall update all sort_order values in a single transaction.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for recipe_steps table.
  • [x] Implement entity, repository, and service with reorder logic.
  • [x] Implement step CRUD and PUT /recipes/{id}/steps/reorder endpoint.
  • [x] Build step list with @dnd-kit/core drag-and-drop.

docs.title: US-5: Comments & Ratings
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to comment on and rate recipes, so that we can share feedback and identify favorites.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 5 hours
  • Dependencies: US-1

Validates Requirements

  • FR-28: Recipe Comments
  • FR-29: Recipe Ratings

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Any household member can add comments.
  2. 1-5 star rating per user (unique constraint).
  3. Display average rating with expandable individual votes (who, score, when).
  4. When a comment is added, create a NEW_RECIPE_COMMENT notification.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for recipe_comments and recipe_ratings tables.
  • [x] Implement entities, repositories, and services.
  • [x] Implement comment and rating endpoints.
  • [x] Build comments section and star rating component with expandable votes.

docs.title: US-6: Meal Time Configuration
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to configure meal times with different schedules per day, so that the planner reflects our actual routine.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 4 hours
  • Dependencies: None

Validates Requirements

  • FR-30: Meal Time Configuration

Acceptance Criteria

  1. CRUD for meal times (Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, etc.).
  2. Configure different times per day of the week (ISO 8601: 1=Monday, 7=Sunday).

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for meals schema, meal_times and meal_time_schedules tables.
  • [x] Implement entities, repositories, and services.
  • [x] Implement CRUD endpoints for meal times with schedules.
  • [x] Build MealTimesConfigPage with per-day time inputs.

docs.title: US-7: Weekly Meal Planner
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to plan weekly meals by assigning recipes to meal times and members, so that our family knows what to eat each day.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 10 hours
  • Dependencies: US-1, US-6

Validates Requirements

  • FR-31: Weekly Meal Plan

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Create weekly plans (Monday–Sunday, unique week_start_date).
  2. Assign one or more recipes per meal entry (multi-recipe meals).
  3. Where a recipe is assigned to specific members, record the user_id. Null means for everyone.
  4. When a user marks an entry as "done", update the is_done flag.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for meal_plans, meal_plan_entries, and meal_plan_entry_recipes tables.
  • [x] Implement entities, repositories, and services.
  • [x] Implement CRUD endpoints for plans, entries, and recipe assignments.
  • [x] Build MealPlannerPage with weekly grid (7 columns × N meal times).

docs.title: US-8: Meal Plan Approval, Thumbs Up/Down & Reminders
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to review meal plans, give feedback, and receive reminders, so that everyone has a say and nobody forgets to cook.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 8 hours
  • Dependencies: US-7, US-10

Validates Requirements

  • FR-32: Meal Plan Approval Workflow
  • FR-33: Meal Preparation Reminders
  • FR-37: Meal Thumbs Up/Down

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When "Notify Household" is clicked, set status to PUBLISHED and create notifications.
  2. When a member accepts, set status to ACCEPTED.
  3. When a member suggests a change, auto-replace their assignment and set status to CHANGED.
  4. Where a meal is done, allow thumbs up/down votes.
  5. Reminder scheduler runs every 15 minutes and creates MEAL_REMINDER notifications.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for meal_plan_votes table.
  • [x] Implement member response and vote services.
  • [x] Implement notify, respond, vote, and done endpoints.
  • [x] Implement MealReminderScheduler (every 15 min).
  • [x] Build approval UI with accept/suggest actions and thumbs up/down buttons.

docs.title: US-9: Shopping List Integration
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to export meal ingredients to a shopping list, so that I can shop for what I need to cook.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 6 hours
  • Dependencies: US-3, US-7

Validates Requirements

  • FR-36: Shopping List Integration

Acceptance Criteria

  1. When export is requested, generate a merge preview (new items + existing items with quantity changes).
  2. When the user confirms, add items to the list or create a new one.
  3. No store assigned to new items unless already in the list.
  4. If an item already exists, increase quantity rather than creating a duplicate.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Implement export preview and merge logic in MealPlanService.
  • [x] Implement preview and export endpoints.
  • [x] Build merge confirmation modal with item summary.

docs.title: US-10: Notifications & Messaging
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to receive notifications and send messages, so that I stay informed and can communicate within the app.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 8 hours
  • Dependencies: None

Validates Requirements

  • FR-38: In-App Notifications
  • FR-39: User Messaging

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Notification bell in header with unread count badge.
  2. Typed notifications (MEAL_PLAN_PUBLISHED, MEAL_REMINDER, MEAL_SUGGESTION_MADE, NEW_RECIPE_COMMENT, NEW_MESSAGE).
  3. When a notification is clicked, mark as read and navigate to the referenced entity.
  4. Direct messaging between household members with read status.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Create Liquibase migration for notifications schema, notifications and messages tables.
  • [x] Implement entities, repositories, and services.
  • [x] Implement notification and messaging endpoints.
  • [x] Build NotificationBell component, NotificationsPage, and messaging UI.

docs.title: US-11: Dashboard Widget & Meals This Week Page
docs.text:
Story Definition

As a household member, I want to see this week's meals at a glance, so that I can quickly check what's planned.

  • Persona: Full-stack Engineer
  • Estimate: 4 hours
  • Dependencies: US-7

Validates Requirements

  • FR-35: Meals This Week View

Acceptance Criteria

  1. Dashboard widget showing today's planned meals with recipe names and assigned members.
  2. Dedicated MealsThisWeekPage with full weekly view.
  3. Current week determined by ISO 8601 Monday.

Implementation Tasks

  • [x] Implement GET /meals/this-week endpoint.
  • [x] Build "Meals This Week" dashboard card.
  • [x] Build MealsThisWeekPage with full weekly grid.
